# Files mentioned by the user:

## COMPOUND-ENGINEERING-HARNESS-HANDOFF.md: /Users/travisgilbert/Downloads/COMPOUND-ENGINEERING-HARNESS-HANDOFF.md

## LIBRARY-ENCODING-PLAN.md: /Users/travisgilbert/Downloads/LIBRARY-ENCODING-PLAN.md

## My request for Codex:
Perfect!! can you commit and push then do the same process with these. again ignore the gates just deliver also I think 'theseus-pro' skill its probably not needed here

items.acceptance_criterion: closing one real run appends the COMPOUND.* events to that run's log, visible in `harness_replay`; replaying the same run appends nothing new.
items.verification: cargo test -p theorem-harness -p theorem-harness-core -p theorem-harness-runtime passed; compound_engineering::tests::run_close_hook_appends_compound_events_and_replay_does_not_double_capture verifies COMPOUND.CAPTURED, COMPOUND.FITNESS_APPLIED, COMPOUND.GATE_PROPOSED, and COMPOUND.DECAYED append after RUN.CLOSED, while RUN.REPLAYED adds no second compound sequence.

items.acceptance_criterion: a qualifying run produces exactly one `auto_triggered` memory whose kind matches its outcome and whose links contain the run id, findable via `recall`; a trivial two-step run produces zero.
items.verification: cargo test -p theorem-harness-runtime --lib passed; compound_engineering::tests::auto_capture_writes_one_memory_and_trivial_run_writes_zero verifies one auto_triggered solution memory for a qualifying positive run and zero memories for a trivial two-step failed run.

items.acceptance_criterion: a positive run that applied pack X shows X's fitness aggregate incremented (visible via `skill_get`); a negative run produces a tension record naming its used items, with no fitness decrement on any of them.
items.verification: cargo test -p theorem-harness-runtime --lib passed; compound_engineering::tests::positive_run_updates_pack_and_memory_negative_run_writes_tension_without_decrement verifies positive pack and memory fitness increments, negative run tension creation, and no positive-count decrement.

items.acceptance_criterion: a shadow pack whose ledger shows a passed gate produces a promotion-proposal record; a memory untouched across the decay window surfaces only under `include_low_fitness` recall; a forced hard-axis regression on a canonical pack produces a demotion plus tension.
items.verification: cargo test -p theorem-harness-runtime --lib passed; compound_engineering::tests::gate_proposal_decay_cluster_and_config_hash_are_data_driven verifies shadow promotion proposals, low-fitness decay with include_low_fitness recall, and rehearsal on retrieval; compound_engineering::tests::canonical_hard_axis_regression_demotes_and_records_tension verifies canonical hard-axis demotion plus tension.

items.acceptance_criterion: two runs with the same task produce captures sharing one cluster key; two unrelated runs do not.
items.verification: cargo test -p theorem-harness-runtime --lib passed; compound_engineering::tests::gate_proposal_decay_cluster_and_config_hash_are_data_driven verifies two same-task runs share one cluster_key and an unrelated task gets a different cluster_key.

items.acceptance_criterion: changing one threshold changes the config hash on subsequent events; no recompile needed to retune.
items.verification: cargo test -p theorem-harness-runtime --lib passed; persist_compound_config stores threshold config as graph data, and compound_engineering::tests::gate_proposal_decay_cluster_and_config_hash_are_data_driven verifies changing capture_step_floor changes subsequent COMPOUND.* config_hash values without recompilation.

items.acceptance_criterion: after both land, exactly one code path folds receipts into fitness, and three real writing-styled runs show StyleReceipt aggregates on the writing pack's fitness.
items.verification: cargo test -p prose-check and cargo test -p theorem-harness-runtime --lib passed; rg found no fold_style_receipts_into_pack_fitness symbol, and style receipts now summarize in writing_style while compound_engineering::apply_usage_fitness owns the single fitness fold path.
